title: Occupant Privacy Perception, Awareness, and Preferences in Smart Office Environments
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eu4xs","text":"Building management systems tout numerous benefits, such as energy efficiency and occupant comfort but rely on vast amounts of data from various sensors. Advancements in machine learning algorithms make it possible to extract personal information about occupants and their activities beyond the intended design of a non-intrusive sensor. However, occupants are not informed of data collection and possess different privacy preferences and thresholds for privacy loss. While privacy perceptions and preferences are most understood in smart homes, limited studies have evaluated these factors in smart office buildings, where there are more users and different privacy risks. To better understand occupants’ perceptions and privacy preferences, we conducted twenty-four semi-structured interviews between April 2022 and May 2022 on occupants of a smart office building. We found that data modality features and personal features contribute to people’s privacy preferences. The features of the collected modality define data modality features – spatial, security, and temporal context. In contrast, personal features consist of one’s awareness of data modality features and data inferences, definitions of privacy and security, and the available rewards and utility. Our proposed model of people’s privacy preferences in smart office buildings helps design more effective measures to improve people’s privacy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xavaw","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Send out email to building listerv to recruit participants without mention of privacy and security,
Send out Qualtrics to provide informed consent and collect gender identity, age group, and role at the university/building space.
Check Qualtrics for completion at the start of the interview.
Review the informed consent.
Interview participants with two interviewers.
Ask questions from the pre-defined list of questions (shown below) and follow up with any interesting points.
What smart devices do you own?
Smartphones? Smart watches?
Devices such as a laptop, tablet?
What was your criteria in deciding what devices to buy?
Refer to the devices they mention
Brand? Capabilities? Convenience/comfort?
[show map of the space they work in]
Could you point out the areas you frequent?
Why? What do you do in those areas?
Which areas would care about data being collected? Why?
What do you mean by care – your perception of caring?
Whether you DON’T want data collected or that you DO?
Refer to the areas they mentioned
What data do you think is being collected in the Link Lab?
Researchers? Facility management? Your supervisor?
If they ask what data, ask well, what type of data do you care about in that area?
Who has access?
How do you feel about that?
Who would you not want to have access? Or be okay with having access?
Under what circumstances would you not be okay with data being collected and analyzed?
Such as type of data, when/time of day,…
How do you feel about data that was collected from you last year? Last month? Compared to today?
Can refer to types of data they mentioned they were not okay with and the concerned areas
What about if the data was collected (say a year ago) and not touched or processed until today?
What if you benefit from the data being collected?
Insights like well-being, monetary compensation
How do your prior answers for (4) and (7)
In the context of smart buildings and the work environment, what is your definition of privacy? security?
Would you be willing to take steps to protect your privacy?
Extra effort/time?
Even if it means taking extra time? 10 min? 30 min?
What is your cutoff/limit?
